howItWorks.intro: This bug-out bag weight calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Weight = Sum of all category weights
Body Weight % = (Total Pack Weight ÷ Body Weight) × 100
Target: 15-20% of body weight for civilian use
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How heavy should a bug-out bag be?
faq.answer: Target 15-20% of your body weight. For a 170 lb person, that's 25-34 lbs. Military standard is 30% for trained soldiers, but civilians should aim lighter. Over 25% severely impacts mobility.

faq.question: What are the essentials for a 72-hour bag?
faq.answer: Priority order: Water (1 gallon/day), shelter (tarp/bivy), fire-starting, first aid, food (2,000+ cal/day), navigation, knife/multi-tool, flashlight, extra socks, rain gear, and documents.

faq.question: How can I reduce bug-out bag weight?
faq.answer: Replace heavy items with ultralight alternatives. Carry a water filter instead of all 3 days of water. Use a tarp instead of a tent. Choose calorie-dense foods. Eliminate duplicates and 'just in case' items.
